data.title: Senate Schedules Vote on SEC Chairman Nominee Paul Atkins
data.description:
U. S. Senate plans a vote on SEC nominee Paul Atkins, impacting cryptocurrency regulation.
source: https://coincu.com/331051-senate-vote-sec-nominee-atkins

data.title: U.S. Senate to Vote on SEC Chair Nominee Paul Atkins
data.description: According to Foresight News, U.S. Senate is set to hold a recess motion vote for Paul Atkins, the nominee for the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair, tomorrow at 11:30 a.m. (UTC+8 today at 11:30 p.m.). The recess motion vote is a crucial step towards confirming his appointment. If successful, a full confirmation vote for Paul Atkins could take place as early as 7:00 p.m. (UTC+8 tomorrow at 7:00 a.m.).

data.title: The whale sold another 5,094 ETH to protect its position, and has lost a total of $40 million on ETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to @EmberCN, the price of ETH has fallen sharply. The whale who was long on ETH through revolving loans sold 5,094 ETH again to avoid liquidation, and exchanged about 7.49 million USDT at an average price of 1,471 US dollars to repay the loan, reducing the liquidation price of Aave positions to 1,246 US dollars. 
Previously, on March 11, the whale also sold 25,800 ETH when ETH plummeted, and the loss was as high as 31.75 million US dollars in a single transaction. The two operations have accumulated a loss of about 40 million US dollars on ETH. 
It is reported that the whale opened a position of ETH through leverage at an average price of 3,084 US dollars in July 2024, and currently holds about 30,000 ETH on Aave, and the risk is still high.

data.title: The U.S. Senate will hold a procedural vote on SEC Chairman nominee Paul Atkins tomorrow
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Eleanor Terrett, a crypto journalist, the U.S. Senate has scheduled a procedural vote on "cloture" (limited debate) for SEC Chairman nominee Paul Atkins at 11:30 a.m. tomorrow (local time). If the procedural vote passes, a formal confirmation vote will be held as early as 7:00 p.m. that evening.

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Clarifies Liability in Cryptocurrency Use
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. Department of Justice has issued a legal memorandum regarding cryptocurrency, clarifying that developers are not liable if their code is used by criminals. The responsibility lies with those who engage in illegal activities using the code. The statement indicates that law enforcement will focus on actual criminal activities such as fraud and terrorism financing. It also emphasizes the protection of legitimate blockchain users and businesses, supporting their fair access to banking services, and reducing enforcement actions against cryptocurrency exchanges and mixing services.

data.title: JPMorgan: A key stock index shows the probability of a U.S. recession is close to 80%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: JPMorgan Chase said that the probability of a recession digested by stocks closely related to the US economy has soared to nearly 80%. At the same time, although funding pressures may intensify, credit product investors remain optimistic. According to JPMorgan Chase's market-based recession indicator dashboard, the Russell 2000 index, which has been hit hard in the recent sell-off, currently reflects a 79% probability of a recession. Other asset classes are also issuing warnings: the S&P 500 index shows a 62% probability of a recession, base metals show a probability of 68%, and the 5-year US Treasury bond has a corresponding probability of 54%. In contrast, the probability of a recession digested by the investment-grade credit market is only 25%, while this probability was zero in November last year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 Futures Experience Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Nasdaq futures saw an early morning drop of 2%, while S&P 500 futures decreased by 1.7%. In post-market trading, MicroStrategy (MSTR) shares fell by 3.13%, and Nvidia (NVDA) shares declined by 3.01%.

data.title: Nasdaq futures fell 2% as Nvidia and other technology stocks fell after hours
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Affected by the continued trade tensions, the Nasdaq futures fell by 2% in early trading, and the S&P 500 futures fell by 1.7%. Many US stocks fell after the market, including Nvidia (NVDA.O) and Pinduoduo (PDD.O) fell by more than 3%, and Apple (AAPL.O) fell by nearly 2%. (Jinshi)

data.title: BTC falls below 76,000 USDT
data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 76,000 USDT and is now at 75,995.6 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 4.18%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: The U.S. Department of Justice makes it clear that encryption developers are not responsible for the misuse of their code
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. Department of Justice issued a cryptocurrency law memorandum, clearly stating that crypto developers are not responsible for their code being used by criminals, and the responsibility should be borne by those who use the code to engage in illegal activities.
The statement shows that the focus of law enforcement will be on actual criminal acts such as fraud and terrorist financing, while emphasizing the protection of the rights and interests of legitimate blockchain users and enterprises, supporting their fair access to banking services, and reducing law enforcement actions against crypto trading platforms and mixing services.
